---
title: ""
page-layout: full
title-block-banner: false
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    toc: false
---

::: {.landing-container}

::: {.snap-panel .hero-panel}
::: {.panel-content}

# In an era of systemic disruption

how do institutions respond to conflict, mistrust, and uncertainty?

Across business and the public sector, the ground is shifting under our feet. We can no longer count on cohesive societies, trust in authority, or a stable rules-based order. What happens when the old models of science, health, and political economy no longer fit the problems they face? My work confronts these 21st century challenges and their implications for academia, government, and industry.

:::
:::

::: {.landing-panel .hero-panel}
::: {.panel-content}




::: {.scroll-cue}
Scroll to explore my research areas.
:::

:::
:::

::: {.landing-panel .research-panel}
::: {.panel-content}

::: {.eyebrow}
Research Area 1
:::

## The Art of Quantitative Research

An introductory project on Bayesian analysis for peace and conflict studies, with attention to research ethics, transparency, open science, and the craft of becoming a researcher.

[Read more](/research/){.btn .btn-outline-primary}

:::
:::

::: {.landing-panel .research-panel}
::: {.panel-content}

::: {.eyebrow}
Research Area 2
:::

## Conflict and American Health in the Turbulent 21st Century

A research agenda on how conflict resolution, behavioral science, and organizational interventions can address medical mistrust, workplace stress, and community health risks.

[Read more](/research/){.btn .btn-outline-primary}

:::
:::

::: {.landing-panel .research-panel}
::: {.panel-content}

::: {.eyebrow}
Research Area 3
:::

## Political Risk in the Era of Economic Statecraft

A research agenda on how geopolitical competition, sanctions, supply chains, industrial policy, and economic statecraft shape organizational and strategic risk.

[Read more](/research/){.btn .btn-outline-primary}

:::
:::

:::
